text: It's never been fully based on album sales. Billboard has continuously changed its scoring metrics since the 1950s to try and keep up with measuring the Hot 100. It's been based on album sales, Nielson metrics of tracking radio spins, iTunes purchases, more recently streaming. They adjust the formula as the industry changes.
label: analysis

text: Before calling the comparison disingenuous, I'm curious what your background is in music sales reporting. Physical sales and streaming are obviously different consumption models, but the industry has long used conversion formulas to measure equivalent commercial activity. You can disagree with the weighting, but acting like the comparison itself is inherently invalid ignores how modern music consumption is actually tracked
label: analysis

text: The album as a whole is different compared to the rest of Kanye's catalog but still works amazingly. Its a timeless album it will work in any setting and has almost no skips. Obviously not Kanyes best work but still amazing.

text: Yeezus im pretty sure literally made guys like Travis Scott Don Toliver and Carti.

text: Fantano said Wale using Afrobeat sounds on his album Shine was inauthentic and fake. Wale is a first-gen Nigerian-American who has collaborated with artists from Africa before. It's hard to imagine a more off-base assessment.

text: I think being a critic only appeals to people who have some sort of ego or belief that they have something to say that nobody else has the capacity to. That applies to pretty much every breadtuber or social critic you can think of.

text: I was thinking about the duality of i and u on TPAB. They describe both the highs and lows of depression. Kendrick talked about how performing i was like an affirmation because it forced him to say kind things and talk about being resilient. As someone who deals with depression that really resonated.

text: I really believe this is still his best album. We Don't Care All Falls Down Spaceship Jesus Walks and Never Let Me Down is such an exceptional way to start off an album. It makes his recent output look so mediocre by comparison just on cohesiveness alone.

text: The four song run from Two Words through Through the Wire through Family Business through Last Call is the best in his discography and one of the best runs of all time.
label: hot_take

text: Album changed the entire trajectory for rap. There's no J Cole Drake Kid Cudi Big Sean Chance Kendrick Lupe ASAP Rocky Travis Scott without the success of this album.
label: analysis

text: Rap was always seen as gangster and thuggish. Kanye changed that and no matter if you like him or not he's probably the most important HipHop artist of all time.
label: hot_take

text: This album is what I tell people to listen to all the way through when people ask why Kanye is considered a genius. Even people that hate his personality now don't really deny that it's endearing and a great album.
label: reaction

text: Kanye was always confident but he's become such an over the top egotistical character that it's hard to believe one of his early appeals was as an everyman. He spoke to those struggling on Spaceships conflicting introspection on All Falls Down and personal family level relationships on Family Business.
label: analysis

text: Hip hop shifted after this album legitimately changed the game in ways few artists have. Also my favourite Kanye album 90% of it has aged well.
label: analysis

text: It's probably his most impactful album I've ever listened to. That's because this is the first album I ever listened to and it got me interested in music.

text: This had a really fresh sound at the time. This really gave a great first look in terms of what was to come from Kanye for the next 15 years.

text: Family Business is top 5 Kanye easy heavily heavily slept on.

text: Still his most perfect album to date. You can literally hear the hard work in it and it has some of the best bars for both Kanye and rap overall.

text: Tied with Late Registration as his best album for me. The raw hunger of this debut album contains so many classic songs and portrays Kanye as an uplifting figure for his community that would go on to influence millions.
